title: Properties (The Data) 📝
description: Give your Structs variables to securely hold their unique information.

slides.title: Filling the Blueprint 📝
slides.content:
# Adding Properties

A variables defined *inside* a Struct is called a **Property**. Let's give our Player some properties!

```swift
struct Player {
    var name: String
    var score: Int
    var isAlive: Bool = true
}
```
Here, every Player we build will be required to have a name and a score. The `isAlive` property defaults to true, so we don't have to provide it every time.

quizData.question: What is the technical name for a variable that lives inside a Struct?
quizData.explanation: Once a variable (`var` or `let`) is placed inside the curly braces of a Type (like a Struct), it is officially called a Property.

slides.title: Building the Object 🏭
Connecting the Dots 🔴
slides.content:
# Dot Notation

To access or change the properties inside your struct instance, you use a period `.` (known as **Dot Notation**).

```swift
var myHero = Player(name: "Cloud", score: 999)

// We use the dot to reach inside the hero and change his score!
myHero.score = 5000
```

quizData.question: How do you access the 'score' property of an instance named 'playerOne'?
quizData.explanation: Dot notation (`instance.property`) is the universal way to 'reach inside' an object and access its data.

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Look at how cleanly we can create completely different items using the exact same Struct blueprint!
